reviews.review_description: First I want to say I really like the controls that are intuitive and fun, and also the music and scenes. However, with that being said—the game is SO difficult. It took me a while to just land on different objects but then the timed trials just killed the game for me. I was thinking of buying the game until that moment. I hate being rushed. I tried and tried but it was just too difficult to get to each place on time and too stressful. Once a game becomes more stressful than fun that kinda ruins it for me.. I like a challenge but not one that’s just too frustrating and that I can’t overcome. I wish there was an “easy” mode or one without the timer trials. Maybe there is and I don’t know about it due to the demo? I’m not sure.. But perhaps I’m not the target audience who is more accustomed to harder or timed games.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words about the controls and music! It means a lot to me that you enjoyed the vibe of the game.

I hear you loud and clear on the difficulty. This was my first game, and I definitely fell into the 'developer trap' - tested it so many times myself that I kept making it harder and harder without realizing it became stressful for everyone else! I'm sorry the timed trials ruined the fun, definitely overcomplicated things as a first-time dev.

This was my first game and I learned a lot. I'm now pouring those lessons into Glitch Metropolis rather than updating this one. Please check out my next move here: glitchmetropolis.com.

reviews.review_description: I'm stuck on the first round. The first red ball keeps pushing me back and I don't know what to do.

developer_response.body: Sorry man. This was my first game, so I thought that its a good idea during tutorial phase to let you find out that you need to hold your hands in front of you - hold - swing back (like paddle in the air) - you move - now you understand.

reviews.review_description: An amazing game using the potential of VR controllers. You have to move your hands to run faster and then put them up to jump. Anyone who likes platform games will love this one.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your kind words!

reviews.review_description: This game is really fun and a good fitness app

This is a really interesting project with a remarkable control system. Especialy after you understand  fundamental thing that you have to keep your hands down and not in front of you. With that, everything works flawlessly. The other mechanics are super simple and intuitive too.

I'm very curious about the full version and the other game mechanics in it. The visuals are super stylish, reminiscent of Monument Valley. I'm glad it's not another wannabe-realistic game. It's just got a flair to it. Definitely check it out!
This game is amazing. Controls are intuitive and more you play it the better it gets. Really looking forward to a full release.

The controls work impressively great! I think this is the first time at all on my experience when movement via hands really implemented well and enjoyable. 
The game features running, jumping, climbing and even pistol actions. 
It’s a great experience like it is. Absolutely impressive demo. 
I think that if put more attractive textures all around this game has all chances to be in top.👍

developer_response.body: Thank you very much! We will try to polish it as much as we can untill release. About the movement, in the full game there will be one extra movement mechanic that will let you control yourself in the air with swinging hands. Hope you will eventually enjoy it.

reviews.review_description:
I love first person vr platformers! I also love Prague. I had to give it a shot.

The worlds are nicely designed. Graphics are simple but crisp and surreal. The central premise of the game is simple - get to the portal - but I found that getting there was almost too easy, at least in the first level in the demo. Maybe this will change in the full version.

Some suggestions:

- The controls are fun and immersive but still pretty rough in some areas. The running motion often feels drifty, like sloshing around in a giant hovercraft, rather than tight and precise. I fell off cliffs to the left or right a couple of times for no apparent reason other than I just slid in that direction while running (or even standing still).

- Make the collectibles smaller! I like being able to see the final portal at the end of a level, but the collectibles should be harder to see (and obtain)

- The worlds are rather small and short. Maybe they'll be bigger in the full release?

- Please add a timer for speedruns!

- In the tutorial, please include a situation in which to demonstrate the time reversal feature. I know that pressing X triggers it, and I hear a whooshing sound, but nothing seems to happen. When could it be useful? What does it do?

Overall, this shows a lot of promise and I'm very excited to see where it goes! Keep up the good work!

developer_response.body:
Hi, 
thank you very much for your feedback!

About the controls: we are still tweaking it.

Size of the worlds: Initial level is supposed to be small, but in a full version we hope to increase the size and play time. Also later in the game there will be a new air control movement that will change the scale of some levels so it will become more spacious.

Timer for speedruns: That is a great idea! We are actually already implementing it and trying to have it done for full version. If you're intersted we can ask you for feedback before final implementation.

Tutorial: We have already changed some of the stuff, but will tweak also.

Thank you for taking the time! We hope that you will like the final result.

reviews.review_title: The game is very cool, jump is wrong
reviews.review_description:
The way you jump is Contra intuitive.
Holding the buttons on both controllers raising your arms and releasing? What? lol on other games you raise your arm hold the button down to pull yourself up or just use A. For me as an adult, I don't have patience for Contra intuitive controllers that won't become second nature because it will always feel like it's inverted and a great movement to climb down a wall not jumping.
 
Either than that is a great game. I would continue to play if it was not the jump issue. I can do it but it throws me off. 
If they come up with controller settings where you can change that I’ll revisit.

Edit-> than the developer actually care! Thank you for the update I'll give it a try again and update the review accordingly
